steps.title: Check for possession
steps.content: The sentence needs a plural noun for the subject, "Joseph Kekuku and other Hawaiian musicians," and a plural noun for the object, "audiences." Neither noun owns anything, so neither should have an apostrophe.

steps.title: Reject apostrophe errors
steps.content:
• Choice B incorrectly makes both "musicians" and "audiences" possessive.
  • Choice C incorrectly makes both nouns singular possessives.
  • Choice D incorrectly makes only "musicians" possessive.
The remaining choice uses plural nonpossessive nouns. The answer is A.
